steps.title: Identify the method of conveying ephemeral nature
steps.content: The passage states Stevens “strove to convey exactly those characteristics of ikaah as a cultural practice” and criticizes the “overly object-focused” view. The strongest signal is how Stevens conveyed the ephemeral and dynamic nature through something other than static objects.

steps.title: Recognize the performative act of transmission
steps.content:
Choice D identifies Stevens's actions as a gift-giver as “temporary and performative,” embodying the ephemeral and dynamic qualities he conveyed.

The performative act of transmission—of both traditional ikaah knowledge and innovative chemistry—reflects Diné dynamism, so the action, not the static object, carries authentic representation.

The others describe a less direct aspect, contradict the passage, or misattribute the cause.

This matches choice D — D is correct.
